newsletters.title: How Anthropic Makes Claude More Reliable
newsletters.content:
Plus: A rapid-fire roundup of AI topics we’ve given ourselves permission to skip, a workflow for onboarding Slack bots, and “Le Chaton Fat” takes center stage  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjowfQ==

Context Window

https://every._bWV0ZXJlZF9wYXl3YWxsPTEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MX0=


HOW ANTHROPIC MAKES CLAUDE MORE RELIABLE https://every._bWV0ZXJlZF9wYXl3YWxsPTEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6Mn0=


PLUS: A RAPID-FIRE ROUNDUP OF AI TOPICS WE’VE GIVEN OURSELVES PERMISSION TO SKIP, A WORKFLOW FOR ONBOARDING SLACK BOTS, AND “LE CHATON FAT” TAKES CENTER STAGE https://every._bWV0ZXJlZF9wYXl3YWxsPTEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6M30=

by Laura Entis https://every._bWV0ZXJlZF9wYXl3YWxsPTEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NH0=

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Living at the edge of AI is bittersweet. You can spend weeks building a workaround to a problem only for a frontier lab to swoop in and solve it for you in a more elegant, reliable way. Today, senior applied AI engineer Nityesh Agarwal https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BuaXR5ZXNoIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjV9 explains how Anthropic’s dynamic workflows feature made his elaborate Claude setup look clumsy in retrospect, the Every team shares which corners of the AI frontier they’ve given themselves permission to ignore, and executive operations manager Jalaiyah Bolden https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5saW5rZWRpbi5jb20vaW4vamFsYWl5YWgtYm9sZGVuLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo2fQ== walks through her step-by-step process for turning a Slack bot into a reliable coworker.

Every is off tomorrow for Juneteenth; we’ll be back Sunday. Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2FjY291bnQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6N30= to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------




MINI-VIBE CHECK: DYNAMIC WORKFLOWS

A CLOSER LOOK AT HOW CLAUDE CODE COORDINATES MULTIPLE AGENTS

When senior applied AI engineer Nityesh Agarwal built Every’s AI project manager https://every. Claudie, he spent days figuring out how to get around the model’s limited context window, or the cap on how much text an LLM can process at once—and the reason Claudie kept dropping key details. His solution: one coordinating agent that delegated tasks to fleets of subagents https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3NvdXJjZS1jb2RlL2NsYXVkZS1jb2RlLWNhbXAiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0=, which gathered data, made updates, and communicated with one another via local markdown files. The process was “a little bit hacky,” Nityesh says, but it worked.

If he were to build Claudie today, he could just use dynamic workflows https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2NvZGUuY2xhdWRlLmNvbS9kb2NzL2VuL3dvcmtmbG93cyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMH0=, Anthropic’s feature for orchestrating large, multi-agent Claude Code tasks. Instead of deciding each step on the fly, Claude writes a reusable script that coordinates the work. It can assign tasks to many subagents and have them check each other’s work before reporting back the results.

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2F0dGlvLmNvbS8_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1ldmVyeVx1MDAyNnV0bV9tZWRpdW09bmV3c2xldHRlcl9zcG9uc29yc2hpcFx1MDAyNnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1ldmVyeS1RMlkyNSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMSwiYWR2ZXJ0aXNlbWVudF9pZCI6MTE4Nn0=




ATTIO IS THE CRM FOR TEAMS THAT SET THE PACE

It compounds every customer signal into context, then acts across your funnel to let you move at unmatched speed and scale. With agents and automations that build pipeline, chase signals, and move deals forward, Attio orchestrates your revenue work around the clock.

Loved by high-growth startups like Granola, Modal, and Wispr Flow, Attio amplifies what you can achieve.

Get started now https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2F0dGlvLmNvbS8_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1ldmVyeVx1MDAyNnV0bV9tZWRpdW09bmV3c2xldHRlcl9zcG9uc29yc2hpcFx1MDAyNnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1ldmVyeS1RMlkyNVx1MDAyNnNvdXJjZT1wb3N0X2J1dHRvbiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMiwiYWR2ZXJ0aXNlbWVudF9pZCI6MTE4Nn0=
Want to sponsor Every? Click here https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEFudGhyb3BpYyBNYWtlcyBDbGF1ZGUgTW9yZSBSZWxpYWJsZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMDcsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJtYWlsdG86c3BvbnNvcnNoaXBzQGV2ZXJ5LnRvIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEzfQ==.

Before dynamic workflows, trying to get Claude to reliably spawn reviewer agents was a persistent headache. Anxious about token spend, the model “would sometimes try to merge it al

newsletters.title: Loops for Non-coders
newsletters.content:
Plus: What to do when your coding model disappears, GitHub's COO on 14 billion agent commits, and engineers questioning their future  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjB9

Context Window

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3ciLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MX0=


LOOPS FOR NON-CODERS https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvbG9vcHMtZm9yLW5vbi1jb2RlcnMiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6Mn0=


PLUS: WHAT TO DO WHEN YOUR CODING MODEL DISAPPEARS, GITHUB'S COO ON 14 BILLION AGENT COMMITS, AND ENGINEERS QUESTIONING THEIR FUTURE https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvbG9vcHMtZm9yLW5vbi1jb2RlcnMiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6M30=

by Laura Entis https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGxhdXJhXzI3YmJhZl8xIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjR9 and Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGthdGllLnBhcnJvdHQxMiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo1fQ==

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veW91dHUuYmUvT0NFVnF5OGtsN1EiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6Nn0= Midjourney/Every illustration.

AI can be exhilarating and destabilizing. Just when you think you have your setup figured out, a powerful new model drops https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjay9hbnRocm9waWMtbXl0aG9zLW91ci1mYWJsZS12aWJlLWNoZWNrIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9—or, in the case of Anthropic’s Fable 5, gets abruptly disabled https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvZmFibGUtZGlzYWJsZWQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OH0=. Today, we explore this instability from multiple angles: Staff writer Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGthdGllLnBhcnJvdHQxMiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo5fQ== maps the grief (and coping mechanisms) that accompanied the Fable ban and shares a practical playbook for the next time a model you depend on disappears, head of growth Austin Tedesco https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQHRlZGVzY2F1IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ== explains how loops are causing him to rethink his approach to working with AI, and GitHub chief operating officer Kyle Daigle tells AI & I https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vcG9kY2FzdCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMX0= guest host Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQG1pa2VfMjExNCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMn0= how the company is responding to an agent-generated surge in commits.



----------------------------------------




‘AI & I’: CAN GITHUB BE FOR EVERYONE?

Today we’re releasing a new episode of our podcast AI & I https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vcG9kY2FzdCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxM30=. Head of tech consulting Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQG1pa2VfMjExNCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNH0= guest hosted this week and spoke to GitHub COO Kyle Daigle about how the company is responding now that everyone—and their army of agents—can ship code.

The volume is extreme: Last year, there were 1 billion commits on GitHub. This year, that figure will safely exceed 14 billion, Daigle says, which puts GitHub in an important but delicate position: It must help developers handle agent-generated code without dictating which pull requests communities should trust or merge.

Watch on X https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veC5jb20vZGFuc2hpcHBlci9zdGF0dXMvMjA2NzI5Mjc3MTUyMjY1NDYyNiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNX0= or YouTube https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veW91dHUuYmUvT0NFVnF5OGtsN1EiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTZ9, listen on Spotify https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vb3Blbi5zcG90aWZ5LmNvbS9lcGlzb2RlLzYyTkpUcnlVaDZEOGlkaGVSWkptMGU_c2k9TkVFNlV2UXpSeW0yam5hazdnRmJVZyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxN30= or Apple Podcasts https://every., or read the transcript https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTG9vcHMgZm9yIE5vbi1jb2RlcnMgIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vcG9kY2FzdC90cmFuc2NyaXB0LWNhbi1naXRodWItYmUtZm9yLWV2ZXJ5b25lIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE5fQ==. And for a behind-the-scenes look at the making of the podcast, check out Mike’s piece https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469ae

==howdy, it’s Barsee again.==

==**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**==

* ChatGPT is about to sound a lot more human

* A developer gave ChatGPT a robot body

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…

## [Speak naturally. Send without fixing.](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/57738193-2774-4169-9924-ee62a48015d9/Newsletter_Hero_AndroidLaunch__9_.png?t=1781704434)
Follow image link: (https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)
Caption: Courtesy: Wispr Flow

Wispr Flow turns your voice into ready-to-send text in any app. Give coding agents 10x more context by speaking instead of typing. Works in Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT, and every IDE.

89% of messages sent with zero edits. Millions of users worldwide. Available on Mac, Windows, iPhone, and Android.

[Try Flow Free](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

##### 1/ ChatGPT is about to sound a lot more human

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/10d60bb6-2da0-46d1-bc93-3a7e52db2a87/HK8qI4RbQAAQxSE__1_.png?t=1781704748)
Caption: 

OpenAI is preparing a major upgrade for ChatGPT Voice built around a new system called [GPT-Bidi](https://www.testingcatalog.com/openai-prepares-major-chatgpt-voice-upgrade-with-gpt-bidi-1/). Unlike today's voice assistants, the model can listen and speak at the same time, allowing ChatGPT to handle interruptions, change its response mid-sentence, and carry on conversations that feel much more natural. References to GPT-Bidi have already appeared across OpenAI's apps, alongside new High, Medium, and Instant intelligence modes that let users choose between deeper reasoning and faster responses.

OpenAI is also [expanding Codex](https://x.com/jxnlco/status/2066970432855581052) with tools that can control desktop apps, interact with websites, and automate workflows across browsers and operating systems. Together, the upgrades push ChatGPT closer to becoming an assistant that can both talk and take action.

**Why it matters:**

Current AI voice assistants still feel unnatural. They pause, wait for their turn to speak, and often struggle when users interrupt them. GPT-Bidi is designed to remove those friction points and make conversations feel more like talking to another person.

Combined with OpenAI's growing push into browser automation and computer control, ChatGPT is moving beyond answering questions and toward completing tasks on a user's behalf.

##### 2/ A developer gave ChatGPT a robot body

Youtube: I Gave ChatGPT a Body (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S67z2aekBrI&utm_source=www.superpowerdaily.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=fix-this-code-the-three-words-that-led-the-u-s-government-to-ban-anthropic-s-fable-and-mythos)

A developer built a low-cost robot called [Growbot](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S67z2aekBrI&utm_source=www.superpowerdaily.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=fix-this-code-the-three-words-that-led-the-u-s-government-to-ban-anthropic-s-fable-and-mythos) by connecting a ChatGPT-like AI model to cameras, sensors, motors, and memory systems. The robot could interpret sensor data, describe physical sensations, write code, perform movements, and even mimic emotions. Memory also allowed it to learn from previous interactions and improve over time.

Despite those capabilities, Growbot still struggled with fine motor control and predicting the physical outcome of its actions. The creator believes the missing piece is a "world model," something similar to the human cerebellum that continuously predicts and adjusts movement in real time.

**Why it matters:**

For years, many people assumed better robots would require dramatically smarter AI. Experiments like Growbot suggest the opposite may be true. Modern language models can already reason, plan, learn, and communicate surprisingly well.

The harder challenge is helping machines understand how the real world behaves. Grabbing a cup, opening a door, keeping balance, or predicting the result of a movement remains far more difficult than generating text. The next breakthroughs in robotics may come from solving those problems rather than building larger models.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Trendos](http://trendos.com/ad-radar?utm_medium=influencers&utm_source=creators&utm_campaign=ai-valle) > 1,000+ brands are buying ads inside ChatGPT right now. Is your competitor one of them? ^~(sponsored)~^

* [Exa Agent](https://exa.ai/blog/exa-agent) > Conduct deep research, build targeted lists, enrich entities, and analyze companies through a single API with structured outputs

* [Okara](https://x.com/askOkara/status/2066899565484413070?s=20) > The world's first influencer agent

* [Origin](https://x.com/swyx/status/2066928345246470204) > Cursor's answer to Git, built to scale for AI-native development and agent workloads

* [Manus](https://x.com/ManusAI/status/2066899447225999515) > Users can now queue messages while long-running tasks are still in progress

* [Innflow](https://innflow.ai/) > Connect your favorite tools and deploy AI agents that automatically handle repetitive workflows.


----------

----------
# WHAT I'M CONSUMING


--------------------
* [Leaked financial docs](https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/06/leaked-financial-docs-show-openai-is-losing-billions-of-dollars-a-year) show OpenAI is losing billions of dollars a year

* New OpenAI [Academy courses](https://openai.com/index/academy-courses-applying-ai-at-work) for the next era of work

* Boston Dynamics Atlas shows signs of [general intelligence](https://interestingengineering.com/ai-robotics/boston-dynamics-atlas-shows-signs-of-general-intelligence-for-factory-work-reports-say) for factory work

newsletters.title: We Built Our Own Agent-native Tool. It Overhauled How We Build Software.
newsletters.content:
A solution for our chaotic Monday meetings changed what we ship customers  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2UgQnVpbHQgT3VyIE93biBBZ2VudC1uYXRpdmUgVG9vbC4gSXQgT3ZlcmhhdWxlZCBIb3cgV2UgQnVpbGQgU29mdHdhcmUuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjB9


WE BUILT OUR OWN AGENT-NATIVE TOOL. IT OVERHAULED HOW WE BUILD SOFTWARE. https://every.


A SOLUTION FOR OUR CHAOTIC MONDAY MEETINGS CHANGED WHAT WE SHIP CUSTOMERS https://every.

by Stella Garber https://every.

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Stella Garber https://every. cofounded Hoop, an AI agent to help subcriber brands cut churn, after years at Trello watching what makes software stick. When her team’s customer discovery calls became a mess of scattered notes and competing interpretations, they built an internal AI analysis tool from scratch using Every’s agent-native architecture https://every. philosophy. It reshaped how they build their actual product. Plus: While you’re waiting for Fable 5 to return, we’ve compiled 13 copy-ready prompts https://every. based on the Every team’s workflows. Use them to plan, build, research, verify, and hand off complex work that runs for hours.—Kate Lee https://every.

Get the Fable prompts https://every.



----------------------------------------



It was Monday morning, and my cofounder Brian was reading from our agent’s weekly analysis of customer discovery calls. “Subscription retention,” he said. “Five separate brands mentioned it as their top priority, and none of them trust existing AI tools to touch it.”

Just weeks ago, unearthing an insight like this would’ve been nearly impossible.

At my pre-product-market fit startup, we’d all been speaking with prospects and trying to figure out the positioning for our product, but keeping track of everything we learned was a mess across founders, platforms, and mediums. To share what we learned during our Monday meeting, Brian would read notes in Slack, collect transcripts from Granola, and try to make sense of it all in Claude Code.

We couldn’t afford to be that disorganized. We’d recently launched Hoop https://every., an agent that helps subscription brands reduce churn, and we needed to learn as quickly as we could. So we had to talk to as many potential customers as possible, then rigorously document and score each call to separate polite interest from genuine demand. Everyone on our five-person team was putting in the effort, but each of us had our own process, our own tools, and our own interpretation of what happened on each customer call.

So my two cofounders and I—none of us with “engineer” in our title—built an internal tool to fix it. What we didn’t expect was that the tool would change how we built our actual product for customers, too.

https://every.




THROW OUT THE OLD PLAYBOOK

The software development lifecycle wasn’t designed for agentic AI—and it shows. Tools, decisions, and the relationship with code itself are shifting faster than any playbook accounts for.

Meet Command Line https://every., Microsoft’s new blog straight from their technical teams, sharing what they’re building, how they’re operating, and what they’re learning along the way. From articles like why the industry keeps confusing AI capability with AI reality to how they’re designing agent-first devices from scratch, Command Line is written by builders, for builders.

Read now https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2UgQnVpbHQgT3VyIE93biBBZ2VudC1uYXRpdmUgVG9vbC4gSXQgT3ZlcmhhdWxlZCBIb3c

Follow image link: (https://www.theaivalley.com/subscribe)
Caption: 

Together with

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/bae2c2e2-901b-4ac1-8306-4fabd5cebb57/adobe-firefly-logo__1_.png?t=1781620040)
Follow image link: (https://news.adobe.com/news/2026/06/creators-toolkit-report-2026)
Caption: 



----------
==howdy, it’s Barsee again.==

==happy tuesday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.==

==**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**==

* Claude Fable 5 was switched off by the US government

* SpaceX acquires AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…


----------

----------
#### **ADOBE**

##### Creative AI is doing more than changing how creators work. It’s also shaping how they think about what’s possible

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3a4935b0-f1ad-44bb-919d-57ff7240c8e7/Screenshot_2026-06-16_at_10.11.48_AM__1_.png?t=1781620024)
Follow image link: (https://news.adobe.com/news/2026/06/creators-toolkit-report-2026)
Caption: Courtesy: Adobe

According to Adobe’s latest Creators’ Toolkit Report, nearly half (48%) of creators say that creative AI makes them feel more secure about their future as creators. In addition, 40% say that AI-assisted content consistently performs better.  Learn more on [Adobe’s newsroom.](https://news.adobe.com/news/2026/06/creators-toolkit-report-2026)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

##### 1/ Claude Fable 5 was switched off by the US government

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/a2eecd65-1474-4ae0-baf7-e848a3184074/Anthropic-Says-US-Gov-Ordered-it-to-Shut-Down-Mythos-Models-Business-2280583841__1_.png?t=1781622310)
Caption: 

More than 100 cybersecurity executives and researchers have [signed](https://freefable.org/) an open letter urging the U.S. government to lift restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. The move comes just days after Anthropic launched Fable 5, its new Mythos-class model designed for general use. On June 12, the company abruptly disabled both models after receiving a government directive restricting access for foreign nationals, including international customers, researchers, and even some Anthropic employees.

The [restrictions](https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access) followed concerns that a jailbreak could allow the models to identify software vulnerabilities and bypass some cybersecurity safeguards. The government order reportedly restricted access for foreign nationals, including international customers, researchers, and even some Anthropic employees. Unable to enforce those restrictions cleanly, Anthropic shut the models off entirely.

**Why it matters:**

Fable 5 went from one of the most anticipated AI launches of the year to being unavailable worldwide in less than three days. That's a level of government intervention the AI industry has rarely seen.

The dispute could shape how future frontier models are released. If advanced AI systems can face restrictions after launch, companies may need to spend as much time working with regulators and national security agencies as they do building the technology itself.

##### 2/ SpaceX acquires AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1c2ee5c6-b667-425a-9f4a-ceb335fbd15d/675664_624347_5551__1_.png?t=1781621236)
Caption: 

SpaceX has agreed to [acquire](https://x.com/BullTheoryio/status/2066836174249852977?s=20) AI coding startup Cursor in a $60 billion stock deal, just days after its blockbuster IPO. Cursor has become one of the fastest-growing software companies in history, with millions of developers using its AI-powered coding tools to write, edit, and manage software. Before the deal, the company was reportedly raising funding at a $50 billion valuation and had attracted backing from Andreessen Horowitz, Thrive Capital, and Nvidia.

**Why it matters:**

SpaceX has told investors that AI could become one of its biggest businesses in the future, with the company estimating a $26 trillion opportunity across AI infrastructure and software. Cursor gives SpaceX a product already used by developers around the world and a direct foothold in one of the fastest-growing parts of the AI market.


--------------------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Runway](https://x.com/runwayml/status/2066596138824696214?s=20) > The AI video generator is now available directly inside OpenAI's ChatGPT

* [Uplift](https://getuplift.ai/) > Connects with your existing tools to build, deploy, and manage custom AI agents that automate repetitive workflows across your team

* [Taste Lab](https://www.tastelab.xyz/) > Analyzes any website's design DNA and transforms it into comprehensive token maps and reasoning briefs for AI-powered agents


----------

----------
# WHAT I'M CONSUMING


--------------------
* Inside Cursor's [wild rise](https://web.archive.org/web/20260616021200/https://www.businessinsider.com/cursor-ceo-michael-truell-spacex-elon-musk-anthropic-2026-6)

* The web we know is going to [disappear](https://www.minid.net/2026/6/15/the-web-is-going-to-dissapear)

* SpaceX & the [Sentient Sun](https://x.com/pmarca/status/2066523671456579828)


----------

----------
# THE VALLEY GEMS


--------------------
__What’s trending on social today:__

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/mntruell/status/2066874098001883538?s=20)

———————————————————————————

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/jamesdouma/status/2066553216817578202?s=20)


----------

----------
# THAT’S ALL FOR TODAY


--------------------
Thank you for reading today’s edition. That’s all for today’s issue.

Plus: Everything we published about Anthropic's new model  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjB9

Context Window

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3ciLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MX0=


FABLE, DISABLED https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvZmFibGUtZGlzYWJsZWQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6Mn0=


PLUS: EVERYTHING WE PUBLISHED ABOUT ANTHROPIC'S NEW MODEL https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvZmFibGUtZGlzYWJsZWQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6M30=

by Every Staff https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQEV2ZXJ5JTIwU3RhZmYiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NH0=

Midjourney/Every illustration.

On Friday night, the U.S. government banned Anthropic’s distribution of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 to non-U.S. nationals. In response, Anthropic disabled Fable for all customers. As of this writing, the situation is ongoing.

It remains to be seen how it will play out, but I can already see the difference in my AI usage. Here’s a graph comparing my Claude and Codex usage before and after the ban (the “event” below):

https://every. courtesy of Dan Shipper.




Before the ban, I was split about evenly between Claude and Codex. After (and after a period where I was using neither because I was sleeping), I switched almost entirely to Codex.

My guess is that this ban is not going to last very long. It seems to rest on a misunderstanding between the government and Anthropic https://every. about which kinds of guardrail bypasses are fixable and what counts as an adequate solution. Anthropic believes the jailbreak identified by the government is narrow rather than universal—it surfaces only minor vulnerabilities that other public models are already susceptible to. The government apparently believes otherwise. Because both sides are highly incentivized to work this out, I’d bet that the ban is revoked after a few days—and demand for the newly returned Fable skyrockets.

However, this kind of move is extremely disruptive and distracting for people working at Anthropic. The only comparable scenario I can remember is Sam Altman https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZXZlcnl0aGluZy90aGlua3NnaXZpbmctaXMtdXBvbi11cyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo3fQ==’s firing https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZXZlcnl0aGluZy90aGlua3NnaXZpbmctaXMtdXBvbi11cyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo4fQ==, which was resolved relatively quickly. Even though Altman was reinstated, I do think the chaos disrupted the company’s momentum for months afterward.
